locations.name: Mount Kailash
locations.notes: Sacred to Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, and Bön. Considered the axis mundi.

locations.name: Uluru
locations.notes: Massive sandstone monolith sacred to the Anangu people and the Pitjantjatjara traditional owners. A UNESCO World Heritage site.

locations.name: Stonehenge
locations.notes: Prehistoric ring of standing stones on Salisbury Plain. Aligned with the solstice sunrise and sunset.

locations.name: Machu Picchu
locations.notes: 15th-century Inca citadel set high in the Andes. A UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.

locations.name: Mount Sinai
locations.notes: Biblical mountain traditionally identified as the site where Moses received the Ten Commandments. Home to the historic Saint Catherine's Monastery.

locations.name: Mount Fuji
locations.notes: Japan's highest peak and an active stratovolcano. Long considered sacred and a frequent subject of pilgrimage and art.

locations.name: Varanasi
locations.notes: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world. Sits on the Ganges and is central to Hindu funerary and devotional practice.

locations.name: Sedona
locations.notes: High-desert town in Arizona known for its red sandstone formations and reputed energy vortexes. A major spiritual retreat destination.

locations.name: Glastonbury Tor
locations.notes: Hill topped by the ruins of a 14th-century church in Somerset. Long associated with Arthurian legend and the myth of Avalon.

locations.name: Mount Shasta
locations.notes: Dormant stratovolcano in Northern California. Long regarded as a sacred mountain and a focal point for spiritual communities.

locations.name: London
locations.notes: Capital of the United Kingdom and a major world city. A common astrocartography reference point for European line work.

locations.name: Tokyo
locations.notes: Capital of Japan and the world's most populous metropolitan area. Frequently used as an East Asian anchor in astrocartography work.

locations.name: Sydney
locations.notes: Largest city in Australia and a frequent Southern Hemisphere astrocartography anchor. Sits on the country's southeastern coast.

locations.name: Rio de Janeiro
locations.notes: Major Brazilian coastal city anchored by landmarks such as Sugarloaf and Christ the Redeemer. A common South American reference point.

locations.name: Cairo
locations.notes: Capital of Egypt and the largest city in the Arab world. Sits on the Nile near the Giza pyramid complex and is a frequent North African anchor.
